parts.title: The Assignment
parts.text: Agent Luna received the message at exactly midnight. She had to be **swift** — there was no time to waste. Her handler Liam confirmed that the mission **fund** had been approved, meaning she had everything she needed. Her training was **solid**, and no one else in the agency could handle this kind of assignment.

parts.title: Into Danger
parts.text: The warehouse was in a **remote** part of the city, far from any road with streetlights. Luna moved with a calm **attitude** — cool and focused, betraying nothing. She preferred to use a small electronic **device** to scan for motion sensors before moving into any new space. A single wrong step could send a **ripple** of alerts through the entire security system. She needed to be **bold** but not reckless.

parts.title: The Vault
parts.text: Inside, she found a safe hidden behind a painting. She used her scanner to crack its combination in seconds. The safe contained documents that could help **establish** the truth about a decades-old theft. A **hump** of folders was stacked neatly on the desk beside it. Luna would **prefer** to photograph everything rather than take originals, and the camera on her watch was more than up to the task.

parts.title: Mission Complete
parts.text: Agent Luna escaped just as the sun rose. The documents revealed a network of stolen art worth millions. Liam met her at the safe house, and his usually **rapid** speech slowed to something almost gentle. "Outstanding work," he said. "You've just solved a case that's been open for fifteen years." Luna allowed herself a small smile. Another mission complete — but she knew the next one was already waiting.

definitions.meaning1: Moving very fast. Example: The swift cheetah caught its prey.
definitions.studentChallenge: The river had a ___ current after the rain.

definitions.meaning1: An amount of money collected and saved for a specific purpose. Example: The class started a fund to raise money for new playground equipment.
definitions.meaning2: To provide money for something (verb). Example: The town voted to fund the construction of a new swimming pool.
definitions.studentChallenge: Students donated their spare change to the ___ for buying books for the school library.

definitions.meaning1: Hard and firm; not liquid or gas. Example: Ice is the solid form of water.
definitions.meaning2: Strong and reliable. Example: She built a solid friendship.
definitions.studentChallenge: The bridge is made of ___ stone.

definitions.meaning1: To set up or create something. Example: They established a new school in the village.
Far away from other places. Example: The village was in a remote area of the mountains.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ island had no roads or electricity.

definitions.meaning1: The way you think or feel about something, shown by your behavior. Example: Having a positive attitude helps you get through tough days at school.
definitions.studentChallenge: Her cheerful ___ made everyone around her feel happy and welcome.

definitions.meaning1: Brave and confident; not afraid. Example: The bold explorer ventured into the cave.
definitions.studentChallenge: It was ___ of her to speak up in front of everyone.

definitions.meaning1: A small wave on the surface of water. Example: A stone thrown into the pond made ripples.
definitions.studentChallenge: The breeze caused a ___ across the lake.

definitions.meaning1: A tool or piece of equipment made for a specific purpose. Example: The tablet is a useful device for reading books, watching videos, and doing schoolwork.
definitions.studentChallenge: The scientist invented a clever ___ that could measure the speed of the wind.

definitions.meaning1: To like one thing more than another. Example: I prefer drawing with coloured pencils rather than painting with watercolours.
definitions.studentChallenge: Do you ___ chocolate or vanilla ice cream for your birthday sundae?

definitions.meaning1: A rounded bump or raised part. Example: A camel has a large hump on its back that stores fat for energy.
definitions.studentChallenge: The car bounced when we drove over the speed ___ in the parking lot.

definitions.meaning1: Very fast; happening in a short time. Example: The rapid growth of the sunflower amazed the class — it grew ten centimetres in just one week.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ current of the river carried the canoe downstream much faster than we expected.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Her cheerful ___ made everyone around her feel happy and welcome.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The ___ explorer ventured into the dark cave without hesitation.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: It was ___ of her to speak up in front of the entire assembly.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: A stone thrown into the pond made small ___ that spread to the edge.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The gentle breeze caused a ___ across the surface of the still lake.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The tablet is a useful ___ for reading books, watching videos, and doing schoolwork.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The scientist invented a clever ___ that could measure the speed of the wind.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: I ___ drawing with coloured pencils rather than painting with watercolours.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Do you ___ chocolate or vanilla ice cream for your birthday sundae?

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: A camel has a large ___ on its back that stores fat for energy on long desert journeys.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The car bounced when we drove over the speed ___ in the school parking lot.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The ___ growth of the sunflower amazed the class — it grew ten centimetres in just one week.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The ___ current of the river carried the canoe downstream much faster than we expected.

secretPassage.instructions: Read the passage below. Fill in each blank with the correct vocabulary word from the word bank.
secretPassage.passage: Agent Luna tracked a stolen painting to a _(1)_ village — a tiny, faraway place with no roads and no phone signal. She crossed a _(2)_ in the path — a rounded rise in the ground — and crouched behind a wall. She used a small electronic _(3)_ — a gadget no bigger than her thumb — to scan for hidden cameras. Her movements were _(4)_, fast and quiet, leaving no trace behind. She needed to _(5)_ her cover story first, setting it up before anyone could question her. The village had used a community _(6)_ — money saved together by the residents — to build a gallery that housed local art. Luna's _(7)_ was calm and confident, showing nothing of the danger she felt inside. The evidence sat on a _(8)_ stone shelf, hard and firm, impossible to move without the right tools. A single wrong step could send a _(9)_ across the still water in the courtyard, alerting the guards. She would _(10)_ stealth over speed, choosing silence rather than rushing. She made a _(11)_ move — brave and daring — and opened the vault door without a sound. The _(12)_ pace of her heartbeat slowed as the painting came into view, exactly where the tip-off said it would be.

wordScaleImposter.studentPick: Pick 2 imposters and explain why they are opposites of the vocabulary word.
